channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Which states approved permanent daylight saving time
item.description: Ten states have enacted legislation to permanently observe daylight saving time should they ever receive federal approval to do so.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, NewsNation

item.title: One month after Charlie Kirk’s murder, key questions remain unanswered
item.description: Several key questions remain unanswered one month after the assassination of Turning Point USA founder Charlie Kirk at Utah Valley University in Orem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: North Korea displays new long-range missile at a military parade celebrating Kim Jong Un
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — At a massive military parade attended by foreign leaders, North Korean leader Kim Jong Un rolled out his nuclear-armed military’s most powerful weapons, including a new intercontinental ballistic missile he may be preparing to test in coming weeks.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: A Sudanese paramilitary attack kills at least 53 people in Darfur, an aid group says
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — A shelling and drone attack by the Sudanese paramilitary forces hit a shelter in a besieged city in the Darfur region, killing at least 53 people, a doctors’ group said Saturday. The onslaught was the latest in Sudan's more than two-year war.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Biden receiving radiation and hormone therapy to treat his prostate cancer
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Former President Joe Biden is receiving radiation and hormone therapy as part of a new phase of treating the aggressive form of prostate cancer he was diagnosed with after leaving office, a spokesperson said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: These states approved permanent daylight saving time: Why their clocks still change in November
item.description: These states have all affirmed that they want to observe daylight saving time year-round, yet their clocks will switch to standard time in November with the rest of us. Here's why.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As the National Guard enters Memphis, memories of MLK and 1968 unrest resurface
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — As National Guard troops arrive in Memphis, the memory of thousands of them with bayonetted rifles and tanks in 1968 is still fresh for Joe Calhoun. Back then, he marched in the streets with sanitation workers and the Rev. Martin Luther King Jr.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN MATTISE and ADRIAN SAINZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans try to weaken 50-year-old law protecting whales, seals and polar bears
item.description: One of the U.S.’s longest standing pieces of environmental legislation, credited with helping save rare whales from extinction, is the subject of an effort for cutbacks from Republican lawmakers who now feel they have the political will to do so.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Whittle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Phoenix Mercury coach Nate Tibbetts ejected from WNBA Finals Game 4 after heated argument
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — Phoenix Mercury coach Nate Tibbetts was ejected from Game 4 of the WNBA Finals after receiving two quick technical fouls in the third quarter against the Las Vegas Aces on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT and DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: A long-lost ancient Roman artifact reappears in a New Orleans backyard
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A New Orleans family cleaning up their overgrown backyard made an extremely unusual find: Under the weeds was a mysterious marble tablet with Latin characters that included the phrase “spirits of the dead.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans is electing a new mayor and a wide field is pledging change after indictments
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — New Orleans voters on Saturday are picking a new mayor to replace two-term incumbent LaToya Cantrell, whose indictment on corruption charges has left a wide field of candidates promising change to city hall.
item.dc:creator: JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Blast at a Tennessee explosives plant leaves 18 missing and feared dead, sheriff says
item.description: McEWEN, Tenn. (AP) — A blast leveled an explosives plant Friday in rural Tennessee, leaving behind a mass of twisted metal, burned-out shells of cars and at least 18 people missing and feared dead, authorities said. “There’s nothing to describe.
item.dc:creator: TRAVIS LOLLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: WIC food program receives $300M to keep running during government shutdown
item.description: A food aid program that supports millions of low-income mothers and their young children received a $300 million infusion from the Trump administration this week, alleviating some anxiety that it would run out of money during the government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Two jurors claim they were bullied into convicting Harvey Weinstein and regret it, his lawyers say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two jurors who voted in June to convict Harvey Weinstein of sexual assault said they regret the decision and only did so because others on the panel bullied them, the former movie mogul’s lawyers said in a newly public court filing.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's quest for the Nobel Peace Prize falls short again despite high-profile nominations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump was passed over for the Nobel Peace Prize on Friday despite jockeying from his fellow Republicans, various world leaders and — most vocally — himself.
item.dc:creator: MIKE PESOLI and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meet your favorite streamers at TwitchCon San Diego 2025
item.description: Video game streamers will take over the San Diego Convention Center for a weekend of meet and greets, interactive workshops, live esports and more at TwitchCon San Diego 2025, set for Oct. 17-19.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, Fox 5

item.title: WGN employee detained amid ICE activity in Chicago, later released
item.description: An employee of WGN-TV was detained by two Border Patrol officers and later released after federal agents swarmed a busy intersection in Chicago Friday Morning.
item.dc:creator: Christine Flores, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Erika Kirk addresses grief in heartfelt social media post one month after husband's assassination
item.description: Charlie Kirk's widow, Erika Kirk, reflected on the grieving process in a social media post, describing the non-linear nature of loss after his Sept. 10 assassination at Utah Valley University.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nor'easter without a name to bring ocean flooding and high winds to US East Coast
item.description: A storm without a name was expected to bring flooding to the East Coast from South Carolina to New Jersey and high winds to New York City over the holiday weekend. While the storm affecting the eastern U.S.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: National Guard stands down in Illinois while troops patrol in Memphis with local police
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — National Guard troops patrolled in Tennessee's second-largest city for the first time Friday while soldiers in Illinois were engaged only in planning and training after a judge handed a loss to the Trump administration and blocked their deployment to the Chicago area.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and CHRISTINE FERNANDO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Most states planning to withdraw National Guard troops from DC this fall
item.description: Nearly half the roughly 2,450 National Guard troops deployed to Washington, D.C., amid President Donald Trump's crackdown on crime are reportedly set to leave the city later this fall.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KRON4

item.title: Arctic seals, more than half of bird species on latest threatened list
item.description: While many animals are increasingly at risk of disappearing forever, the updated list shows how species can come back from the brink with dedicated effort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pope urges news agencies to stand as bulwark against lies, manipulation and post-truths
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Leo XIV encouraged international news agencies on Thursday to stand firm as a bulwark against the “ancient art of lying” and manipulation, as he strongly backed a free, independent and objective press.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, ABC 4

item.title: A history of President Trump's legal battles with New York Attorney General Letitia James
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The day she was elected New York's attorney general, Letitia James blasted President Donald Trump as a “con man” and “carnival barker" and pledged to scrutinize his public policies and personal business dealings.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tesla self-driving software under investigation after traffic violations
item.description: The NHTSA pointed to 18 complaints and one media report in which Tesla vehicles using the self-driving software failed to remain stopped or to stop fully at a red light or failed to accurately detect the traffic signal.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Tennessee teacher fired over Charlie Kirk posts files free speech lawsuit
item.description: A longtime Rutherford County teacher is suing her school district, claiming she was fired for what she shared online.
item.dc:creator: Kelly Milan, ABC 4

item.title: Pro-Palestinian agitators hit with felony 'promotion of anarchy' charges after riot in Dem-run city: officials
item.description: Agitators arrested in Tuesday night's pro-Palestinian riot now face a felony “promotion of anarchy" charge, according to the district attorney's office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Spanberger, Earle-Sears face off in Nexstar's Virginia gubernatorial debate
item.description: Former U.S. Congresswoman Abigail Spanberger and current Virginia Lt. Gov. Winsome Earle-Sears squared off Thursday evening in their only scheduled live debate in the race for Virginia governor.
item.dc:creator: Jane Alvarez-Wertz, NewsNation

item.title: Turning Point USA announces 'All American' Super Bowl halftime show
item.description: Turning Point USA, the grassroots conservative political action network founded by the late activist Charlie Kirk, is planning a Super Bowl halftime show with the goal of competing with the official show featuring Bad Bunny and sponsored by Apple Music.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WGN 9

item.title: Trump expects Israeli hostages to be released on Monday
item.description: President Donald Trump says he expects the remaining hostages to be released from Gaza on Monday as part of the first phase of Trump's 20-point peace plan.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: St. Petersburg to vote on flags for LGBTQ+ and Black communities after DeSantis erases murals
item.description: ST. PETERSBURG, Fla. (AP) — The Florida city of St. Petersburg, long a bastion of LGBTQ+ rights and mindful of its racially segregated past, is considering approval of two flags recognizing those issues after Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: VIDEO: Monarch butterfly with broken wing gets wing transplant
item.description: A monarch butterfly with a broken wing was miraculously able to fly again after it underwent a wing transplant at a nature preserve.
item.dc:creator: Katlyn Fernandez, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump to undergo medical check-up on Friday
item.description: While the White House said Friday's visit was a "routine yearly check up," it will be the second time Trump has had an exam at Walter Reed so far this year.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WGN 9

item.title: Nobel Prize in literature awarded to Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai
item.description: Hungarian writer László Krasznahorkai, whose philosophical, bleakly funny novels often unfold in single sentences, won the Nobel Prize in literature Thursday for his for his "compelling and visionary oeuvre that, in the midst of apocalyptic terror, reaffirms the power of art.
item.dc:creator: KOSTYA MANENKOV and MIKE CORDER Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tropical Storm Jerry could become hurricane Friday: NHC
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (WFLA)— Tropical storm conditions are possible today and into Friday as Tropical Storm Jerry passes by the northern Leeward Islands, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New California law aims to improve school nutrition by phasing out some ultraprocessed foods
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California will phase out certain ultraprocessed foods from school meals over the next decade under a first-in-the-nation law signed Wednesday by Gov. Gavin Newsom.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4
